moveAnnotations.annotation: We play the Saragossa Opening, an unusual but solid first move that immediately prepares to support the center with d4. By controlling d4 before moving our pawn there, we gain flexibility and avoid allowing Black to quickly challenge a lonely d-pawn. This move is less common than 1.e4 or 1.d4, which gives it a practical advantage: Black must think for themselves rather than fall back on well-known responses. We're aiming for a slow, positional game where we build our center patiently and maintain piece coordination—notice how c3 also opens the long diagonal for our light-squared bishop and prepares queenside flexibility.
